intro: Lotononis lupinifolia, also known as the "Karee bush", is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to southern Africa, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing fever, inflammation, and pain, as well as treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Lotononis lupinifolia include alkaloids, saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
